title: fraxinus caroliniana var. pauciflora
intro: Fraxinus caroliniana var. pauciflora, also known as the Carolina ash or small-flowered Carolina ash, is a variety of deciduous tree native to the southeastern United States. The medicinal properties of Fraxinus caroliniana var. pauciflora include its bark, which contains saponins and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant include its potential use in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Fraxinus caroliniana var. pauciflora can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
